mechanism: Output gap near zero (-0.5% to +0.5%) means small shocks can push the economy in either direction, Phillips Curve is at maximum steepness (convexity), and supply shocks have maximum impact because no demand shortfall absorbs them. This structural configuration maximizes correlation instability.
regime_dependence: The instability amplification is specific to near-zero output gaps. Deeply negative gaps (recession) reliably produce negative correlation; deeply positive gaps with inflation produce positive correlation.
